# timor_demo — synthetic Timor village-DRE test case

**Everything in this folder is synthetic.** It is generated by
`tools/make_timor_demo.py` and exists so the village workflow can be tested,
taught, and demonstrated before real Timor data is assembled. Do not use it
for policy results.

What it represents (stylised, NTT-plausible):

- **Grid**: a two-zone Timor system — zone 1 ≈ Kupang area (coal at Bolok, a
  gas-engine peaker, diesel, 5 MW existing solar), zone 2 ≈ the Soe/Kefamenanu
  interior (diesel only), connected by one 20 MW corridor that can be
  reinforced. ~132 MW existing capacity against a ~71 MW system peak (the
  concept note's Timor system is 181 MW / 71 MW).
- **Villages**: four off-grid clusters (amfoang, boking, raijua, wini), each
  with an existing village diesel genset (0.15–0.5 MW), candidate solar PV and
  battery storage, an evening-peaked load (peaks 0.05–0.15 MW), and zero heat
  demand.
- **Time**: 8 uniform representative weeks (1,344 hours, weights summing to
  8760), a smooth solar profile with mild dry/wet-season variation, and diesel
  priced at $18/MMBtu so solar-plus-storage competes the way it does in real
  isolated NTT systems.

Run it with:

```bash
python generate_jobs_local.py \
  --scenarios-file scenario_timor_demo.yml \
  --run-script run_model.jl \
  --output-root jobs
```

which solves the `village` (standalone) and `gridvillage` (coordinated)
scenarios — the comparison at the core of the village solar study.

To change assumptions (loads, costs, profiles, number of villages), edit
`tools/make_timor_demo.py` and re-run it rather than editing the CSVs by hand.
